1	# Story 6: Artifact Listing — Search, Sort, Filter (FINAL)
2	
3	## Diagnosis (current state)
4	
5	**Backend** (`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_artifacts.go`, `schema.graphql:1950-2230`):
6	- `artifacts(projectID, first/after/last/before, mediaType, search)` already supports:
7	  - Server-side `mediaType` — **exact equality** (resolver line 56), so the UI's `image/*` chip currently matches nothing. Existing bug.
8	  - Server-side `search` over **title + path** (case-insensitive substring).
9	  - Default sort: by `Path` ascending, hard-coded.
10	- Artifact node fields available for sort/filter: `id`, `path`, `title`, `mediaType`, `staleness`, `updatedAt`, `ddxFrontmatter` (JSON), `description`, `generatedBy`.
11	- **Adjacent infra discovered**: `Query.search` is in the schema with `SearchResult.snippet`, but its resolver still panics (`resolver.go:136`). Command palette has reusable scoring/token matching (`resolver_feat008.go:415,1042`). Document detail has client-side overlay search (`documents/[...path]/+page.svelte:17`). Story 6 should reuse the `SearchResult`/snippet contract rather than invent a new one.
12	- Cursor encodes only artifact ID; sort changes need client to drop `after` and resolver needs deterministic tie-breaker.
13	
14	**Frontend** (`routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.{ts,svelte}`):
15	- Filter chips for `mediaType` (URL-persisted, server-side, triggers reload).
16	- Search box: client-side only over already-loaded edges; URL-persisted via `replaceState`; **does NOT pass `search` to GraphQL** — so search misses anything past the loaded cursor page.
17	- No sort UI. No staleness filter. No phase/folder/ID-prefix filter. No snippet rendering.
18	- Reusable URL/load-more patterns exist in beads page (`beads/+layout.svelte:112`).
19	
20	**FEAT-008** already requires (US-081b at line 390, US-084 at line 539): category chips + URL state (mediaType chips done), full-text across textual content + sidecar metadata + frontmatter (NOT done), snippet highlighting (NOT done).
21	
22	## Spec changes
23	
24	**FEAT-008-web-ui.md** — extend US-081b acceptance with sort dropdown, staleness chip, multi-facet composition, URL-state contract; promote US-084 with concrete server-side scope (title+path → +description+frontmatter → +body) and snippet contract via existing `SearchResult.snippet`. Avoid importing the beads "client-side, no round-trip" search language (US-082b at line 440); artifact list is cursor-paginated and must be server-side.
25	
26	**TD-NNN — artifact full-text search & facet contract** (new): scope decision for content indexing, size caps, text/binary skip rules, deterministic ordering with tie-breaker, and the **facet contract Story 5 must conform to** (URL keys: `q`, `mediaType`, `staleness`, `phase`, `prefix`, `sort`; multi vs single select per facet; GraphQL arg names).
27	
28	## Implementation surface
29	
30	### Phase 1 — wire existing capability + sort + staleness (cheap)
31	- Frontend `q` → GraphQL `search` arg (kill client-side fallback so search covers full set, not just loaded page).
32	- Fix `mediaType` wildcard semantics: either translate `image/*` to a prefix match in resolver, or remove wildcard chip.
33	- Add GraphQL `sort: ArtifactSort` enum: `PATH_ASC|PATH_DESC|TITLE_ASC|TITLE_DESC|UPDATED_DESC|UPDATED_ASC|STALENESS`. Every sort uses `(sortKey, id)` tie-breaker.
34	- Add `staleness: String` arg.
35	- Frontend: sort dropdown, staleness chip row, URL state for all params, single source of truth in `+page.ts`. Client clears `after` whenever any of `sort/q/mediaType/staleness` changes.
36	
37	### Phase 2 — metadata search (cheap-medium)
38	- Extend server `search` to also scan `description` and `ddxFrontmatter` JSON (frontmatter summary already exposed for docs at resolver_artifacts.go:207). No new index.
39	
40	### Phase 3 — full-text body + snippets (TD-gated)
41	- Reuse existing `SearchResult.snippet` shape. Add `ArtifactEdge.snippet: String`.
42	- On-the-fly grep over textual artifacts at query time, size cap (e.g. 256 KB/file), skip binary, deterministic ordering. Document upgrade path to bleve/sqlite-FTS when artifact count exceeds threshold.
43	- Frontend: snippet rendering with highlighted match terms.
44	
45	## Acceptance Criteria
46	
47	- Given I type a query, the search hits the **entire** project (not just loaded page); URL persists `q=`.
48	- Given I select a sort option, the list reorders, `sort=` persists, pagination resets cleanly with deterministic tie-breaker.
49	- Given I apply staleness=stale, only stale artifacts show; chip state persists.
50	- Given I combine `mediaType + staleness + sort + q`, all four compose; pasting the URL reproduces the same view.
51	- Given I clear all filters, URL resets to `/artifacts`.
52	- Given the Images chip is selected, `image/png`/`image/svg+xml` artifacts both appear (wildcard fix verified).
53	- Given Story 5 lands axes (`phase`, `prefix`), they slot into the published facet contract without re-architecture.
54	- (Phase 3) Given a query matches body text, I see the artifact with a highlighted snippet.
55	
56	## Tests
57	
58	**Unit (Go):** `resolver_artifacts_test.go` — sort orderings + tie-breaker stability, staleness filter, search composition (title/path/description/frontmatter), wildcard mediaType match, pagination correctness across filtered+sorted set.
59	
60	**Unit (Vitest):** URL-state round-trip (parse/serialize) for all params; sort dropdown emits expected query; load-more preserves all query state.
61	
62	**E2E (Playwright):** `artifacts.spec.ts` — server-side search matches across pages; sort change resets pagination; staleness chip filters; URL copy/reload restores state; (Phase 3) snippet highlight visible.
63	
64	**Perf (Go bench):** B4a benchmark — 500-artifact fixture with size caps, p95 budget for content search; verify worst-case stays bounded.
65	
66	## Risks
67	
68	- **Cursor + filter/sort interaction:** server-side filters/sort invalidate cursors. Mitigation: client drops `after` on any param change; resolver enforces `(sortKey, id)` tie-breaker.
69	- **Wildcard chip currently broken** — must be fixed in B1, not deferred.
70	- **Story 5 coupling:** must publish facet contract NOW (in TD-NNN) so Story 5 grouping names don't drift from Story 6 filter URL keys.
71	- **Search semantics:** simple substring v1; boolean/regex out of scope; document the choice.
72	- **B4 size:** split into B4a/B4b/B4c (TD+server, GraphQL contract+resolver, frontend+e2e).
73	
74	## Bead Breakdown
75	
76	| # | Title | Deps | Tier |
77	|---|-------|------|------|
78	| B1 | Fix mediaType wildcard match + wire frontend `q` to server `search` arg + add `sort` enum and `staleness` arg with tie-breaker | none | cheap (deterministic) |
79	| B2 | Frontend sort dropdown + staleness chips + URL state contract for all filter params; client resets `after` on any param change | B1 | cheap |
80	| B3 | Publish facet contract (TD-NNN section) + add `phase` / `prefix` filter args + chips wired to Story 5 axes | Story 5 final, B2 | medium |
81	| B4a | TD-NNN: artifact search semantics (title→path→description→frontmatter→body), size caps, binary skip, deterministic ordering, perf benchmark fixture | B1 | strong (design + measurement) |
82	| B4b | GraphQL: extend `search` to metadata fields + add `ArtifactEdge.snippet` (reuse `SearchResult.snippet` shape) + resolver tests | B4a | medium |
83	| B4c | Frontend snippet rendering + highlight + e2e for full-text path and back-navigation | B4b | cheap-medium |
84	
85	Suggested execution: B1 → B2 in fast lane; B4a kicks off in parallel as a TD; B3 waits on Story 5 final; B4b → B4c gated by B4a.
86	
87	## Coordination with Story 5
88	
89	Story 5 defines categorization axes (folder, frontmatter, HELIX phase). Story 6 turns axes into filter facets.
90	
91	**Action item from codex review:** Story 6 publishes a facet contract NOW (in TD-NNN, before B3) so Story 5 cannot drift:
92	- URL keys reserved by Story 6: `q`, `mediaType`, `staleness`, `phase`, `prefix`, `sort`.
93	- Multi vs single select: `mediaType` single (today), `staleness` single, `phase` single, `prefix` multi.
94	- Source fields: `phase` derived from path prefix `docs/helix/NN-*/`; `prefix` derived from `id` prefix segment (`ADR|SD|FEAT|US|RSCH|...`).
95	- Filter narrows; Story 5 grouping organizes the remaining set. Both compose without collision.
96	
97	## Flagged ambiguities (unresolved)
98	
99	1. **Search scope final v1:** title+path only (today), or title+path+description+frontmatter? Recommendation: ship title+path (B1) then immediately metadata (B4b) — only body text is gated by TD.
100	2. **Server vs client execution:** decided **server-side** (cursor pagination forces it). Documented.
101	3. **URL state persistence:** decided **deep-linkable URL** (no session-only state). Documented.
102	4. **Boolean/regex search:** out of scope v1. Substring only.
103	5. **Wildcard mediaType:** fix-in-place (translate `image/*` to prefix in resolver) or drop chip — needs decision in B1.
104	6. **Phase axis source of truth:** path-prefix derivation vs explicit frontmatter field — needs Story 5 alignment.
105

1	import type { PageLoad } from './$types'
2	import { createClient } from '$lib/gql/client'
3	import { gql } from 'graphql-request'
4	import { readState } from '$lib/urlState'
5	import type { GroupBy } from './grouping'
6	
7	export const ARTIFACTS_QUERY = gql`
8		query Artifacts($projectID: ID!, $first: Int, $after: String, $mediaType: String, $search: String, $sort: ArtifactSort, $staleness: String) {
9			artifacts(projectID: $projectID, first: $first, after: $after, mediaType: $mediaType, search: $search, sort: $sort, staleness: $staleness) {
10				edges {
11					node {
12						id
13						path
14						title
15						mediaType
16						staleness
17					}
18					cursor
19				}
20				pageInfo {
21					hasNextPage
22					endCursor
23				}
24				totalCount
25			}
26		}
27	`
28	
29	export interface ArtifactNode {
30		id: string
31		path: string
32		title: string
33		mediaType: string
34		staleness: string
35	}
36	
37	export interface ArtifactEdge {
38		node: ArtifactNode
39		cursor: string
40	}
41	
42	export interface PageInfo {
43		hasNextPage: boolean
44		endCursor: string | null
45	}
46	
47	export interface ArtifactConnection {
48		edges: ArtifactEdge[]
49		pageInfo: PageInfo
50		totalCount: number
51	}
52	
53	interface ArtifactsResult {
54		artifacts: ArtifactConnection
55	}
56	
57	export const PAGE_SIZE = 50
58	
59	export const SORT_OPTIONS = [
60		{ value: 'PATH', label: 'Path' },
61		{ value: 'TITLE', label: 'Title' },
62		{ value: 'MODIFIED', label: 'Last modified' },
63		{ value: 'DEPS_COUNT', label: 'Dependency count' },
64		{ value: 'ID', label: 'ID' }
65	] as const
66	
67	export type ArtifactSort = (typeof SORT_OPTIONS)[number]['value']
68	export const DEFAULT_SORT: ArtifactSort = 'PATH'
69	
70	const SORT_VALUES = new Set(SORT_OPTIONS.map((o) => o.value))
71	
72	export function parseSort(raw: string | null): ArtifactSort {
73		return raw && SORT_VALUES.has(raw as ArtifactSort) ? (raw as ArtifactSort) : DEFAULT_SORT
74	}
75	
76	export const STALENESS_OPTIONS = ['fresh', 'stale', 'missing'] as const
77	export type Staleness = (typeof STALENESS_OPTIONS)[number]
78	const STALENESS_SET = new Set<string>(STALENESS_OPTIONS)
79	
80	export function parseStaleness(raw: string | null): Staleness | null {
81		return raw && STALENESS_SET.has(raw) ? (raw as Staleness) : null
82	}
83	
84	export const load: PageLoad = async ({ params, url, fetch }) => {
85		const state = readState(url.searchParams)
86		const mediaType = state.mediaType
87		const q = state.q
88		const groupBy: GroupBy = state.groupBy
89		const sort = parseSort(state.sort)
90		const staleness = parseStaleness(state.staleness)
91	
92		const client = createClient(fetch as unknown as typeof globalThis.fetch)
93		const data = await client.request<ArtifactsResult>(ARTIFACTS_QUERY, {
94			projectID: params.projectId,
95			first: PAGE_SIZE,
96			mediaType: mediaType ?? undefined,
97			search: q ? q : undefined,
98			sort,
99			staleness: staleness ?? undefined
100		})
101		return {
102			nodeId: params.nodeId,
103			projectId: params.projectId,
104			artifacts: data.artifacts,
105			mediaType,
106			q,
107			groupBy,
108			sort,
109			staleness
110		}
111	}
112

# Location of all your GraphQL files (relative to this config file's directory)
schema:
  - schema.graphql

# Location where the generated server code will be written
exec:
  filename: generated.go
  package: graphql

# Configuration for resolvers generated by gqlgen
resolver:
  filename: resolver.go
  package: graphql
  type: Resolver

# Location of the models
model:
  filename: models.go
  package: graphql

  "bead_id": "ddx-5af9ae07",

  "no_changes_rationale": "Bead ddx-5af9ae07 — no source changes this attempt; work is already merged.

Status: All 4 acceptance criteria are satisfied by code already present in HEAD.
The bead was reopened due to an incorrect post-merge review on a prior attempt
(result_rev=dd0b14d2, base_rev=a69e071b). That diff in fact contained 369 lines
of source changes across 6 files (verified via `git diff --stat
a69e071b..dd0b14d2`); the reviewer's claim that it "contains only execution
evidence" was wrong. The implementation landed on main as commit:

  d0a5dec0 feat(web): artifacts sort dropdown + staleness chips, cursor reset
           on param change [ddx-5af9ae07]

This commit is an ancestor of the current worktree HEAD (verified via
`git merge-base --is-ancestor d0a5dec0 HEAD`), so the contract is already met.

AC coverage in current HEAD:

AC1 — Sort dropdown visible; URL state round-trips.
  * Source: cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.svelte:262-274
    (<select data-testid="sort-select"> populated from SORT_OPTIONS).
  * URL plumbing: cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/urlState.ts:43-68
    (writeState writes/clears `sort`); +page.ts:84-110 (load() reads `sort`
    via readState and parseSort).
  * Tests:
    - Unit: cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/urlState.test.ts:57-67
      ("round-trips sort and staleness, and clears them on null").
    - E2E: cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/artifacts.spec.ts:316-322
      (selectOption('TITLE') -> URL gains ?sort=TITLE, refetch issued with
      variables.sort === 'TITLE').

AC2 — Staleness chips (fresh/stale/missing) toggle filter.
  * Source: +page.svelte:221-245 ([data-testid=staleness-chips], per-value
    [data-testid=staleness-chip-{value}], aria-pressed reflects active
    state, re-click toggles off via selectStaleness in :100-104).
  * Constants: +page.ts:76-82 (STALENESS_OPTIONS = ['fresh','stale','missing'],
    parseStaleness).
  * E2E: artifacts.spec.ts:325-334 (toggle stale, URL gains
    ?staleness=stale, refetch sees variables.staleness==='stale'); :349-354
    (toggle off removes the param and refetch sees staleness undefined).

AC3 — Param change resets cursor.
  * Source: +page.svelte:67-78 — navigateWith() calls goto(), which re-runs
    load(); the $effect at +page.svelte:30-33 reassigns allEdges and
    pageInfo from the fresh data, dropping the prior endCursor. The
    fetchSeq.invalidate() in onSearchInput (:80-89) drops stale loadMore
    responses.
  * E2E: artifacts.spec.ts:320-322, :330-332, :340-347 — every refetch
    after a sort/staleness/search change asserts `last.after` is null,
    proving the cursor was reset.

AC4 — e2e covers sort/filter/search composition.
  * artifacts.spec.ts:222-355 — single test that walks through:
    initial render -> change sort -> toggle staleness chip -> add search ->
    clear staleness, asserting URL composition and that each request carries

data.text: Now let me write the rationale and commit.

input.content:
# ddx-8ec3e08e — AC evidence

The bulk of B1 was already merged in HEAD before this attempt:

- `13896420 fix(artifacts): mediaType wildcard + server-side search + sort/staleness args [ddx-0ac5f35d]`
- `d0a5dec0 feat(web): artifacts sort dropdown + staleness chips, cursor reset on param change [ddx-5af9ae07]`
- `8d1edfa4 fix(web): drop stale artifacts loadMore responses on search race [ddx-18b15451]`

This attempt strengthens AC2 (`each value sorts deterministically with id tie-breaker (resolver test)`) by adding `TestArtifacts_SortAllValuesTieBreak`, which exercises every `ArtifactSort` enum value (`ID`, `PATH`, `TITLE`, `MODIFIED`, `DEPS_COUNT`) and asserts the `(sortKey, id)` tie-breaker. Prior coverage only exercised `TITLE`.

## AC mapping

- **mediaType `image/*` matches `image/png` and `image/svg+xml`** —
  resolver: `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_artifacts.go:55-61` (HasPrefix on the trimmed prefix). Test: `TestArtifacts_MediaTypeWildcard` (`cli/internal/server/graphql/artifacts_test.go:161`).
- **`ArtifactSort` enum present; deterministic tie-breaker per value** —
  schema: `cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:2001-2013`. Resolver tie-breaker: `resolver_artifacts.go:169-196` (`a.ID < b.ID` fallback). Tests: existing `TestArtifacts_SortByTitleStable` plus new `TestArtifacts_SortAllValuesTieBreak` covering all five enum values.
- **staleness filter narrows the result set** —
  resolver: `resolver_artifacts.go:73-82`. Test: `TestArtifacts_StalenessFilter`.
- **Frontend artifacts page calls GraphQL with `search` from URL `q` (no client-side filter)** —
  load: `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.ts:84-110` (passes `search: q ? q : undefined`). Page: `+page.svelte:55` (`const filtered = $derived(allEdges)` — no client-side filtering).
- **Changing `q`/`sort`/`mediaType`/`staleness` clears `after`** —
  `+page.svelte:67-78` `navigateWith()` calls `goto()`, which re-runs `load()`; the `$effect` at `+page.svelte:30-33` reassigns `allEdges` and `pageInfo` from fresh data, dropping the prior `endCursor`. `loadMore` always reads the current `pageInfo.endCursor` after that reset.

## Schema enum naming note

The bead description proposes enum values `PATH_ASC|PATH_DESC|TITLE_ASC|TITLE_DESC|UPDATED_DESC|UPDATED_ASC|STALENESS`. The actual enum that landed in `13896420` is `ID|PATH|TITLE|MODIFIED|DEPS_COUNT` — a single ascending direction per key with `id` as the deterministic tie-breaker. The AC text only requires "ArtifactSort enum present in schema; each value sorts deterministically with id tie-breaker", which the existing names satisfy. Renaming now would break the already-merged frontend dropdown (`d0a5dec0`) and the subsequent sub-bead B2; that re-architecture belongs in a follow-up bead, not in B1.

## Test verification

- `cd cli && go test -count=1 -v ./internal/server/graphql/... -run TestArtifacts` — PASS (8/8, all 5 sub-tests of `SortAllValuesTieBreak` green).
- `cd cli && go test -count=1 ./internal/server/graphql/...` — PASS.
- `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run test:unit -- --run src/routes/nodes src/lib/urlState` — PASS (32/32).
- The full `bun run test` has one pre-existing failure in `src/lib/components/D3Graph.contrast.spec.ts` (asserts the graph component avoids `stroke-opacity`); that file is unrelated to the artifacts list and the failure is present in HEAD before this attempt.

data.text: Committed `16aaf8b`. The bead's surface area was already merged in HEAD via prior commits (`13896420`, `d0a5dec0`, `8d1edfa4`); this attempt closes the gap on AC2 by adding `TestArtifacts_SortAllValuesTieBreak`, which exercises every `ArtifactSort` enum value (`ID`, `PATH`, `TITLE`, `MODIFIED`, `DEPS_COUNT`) and asserts the `(sortKey, id)` tie-breaker. Evidence and per-AC mapping are in `.ddx/executions/20260502T133823-f8df7c6d/ac_evidence.md`, including a note that the bead's proposed `*_ASC/*_DESC` enum names diverge from the names that actually shipped — renaming would break the already-merged frontend and B2 and is best handled as a follow-up.
